extraction.one_line_summary: BUITRAGO Santiago won from the break on Tre Cime, while ROGLIC attacked THOMAS inside 1km to go, gained only 3 seconds, leaving GC margin at 26s before final mountain TT
tactical_patterns: ROGLIC switched to near-mountain bike gearing (44t rear) pre-climb, signaling expected maximum-gradient sustained efforts rather than tempo riding.
THOMAS counterattacked with <300m remaining for bonus seconds after defending ROGLIC move, showing willingness to gamble pink jersey defensively.
Breakaway rider COURT staying ahead meant only 3-second gap materialized despite late GC attack, bonus seconds gambit backfired tactically.

rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: positive: attacked inside final kilometer on 20% gradients but extreme gearing choice suggests concern about sustaining power on steepest pitches
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: positive: sat on AUDENAERT then ALMEIDA wheels all climb, countered late for bonus seconds showing confidence despite small margin
rider_signals.BUITRAGO Santiago: positive: caught GREE with 1.5km remaining and rode straight past, high-altitude native advantage materializing on Cima Coppi
rider_signals.GREE Derek: negative: caught and dropped by BUITRAGO despite being in break all day, unable to close stage win despite multiple attempts
rider_signals.ALMEIDA Joao: negative: lost almost a minute to podium placing, dropping to nearly 1 minute behind THOMAS
